Finishing Your Bandana

Back to Crochet Workshop Resources main page

Resources

  • Bandana pattern – the pattern you used in class
  • Purl Soho Crochet Basics – quick reference for most skills in this pattern
  • Wool and the Gang crochet tutorial library – videos on a range of crochet skills used in Wool and the Gang patterns

Helpful Videos

  • Learn crochet as a beginner
  • How to hold your hook
  • How to: Crochet Turning Chain
  • How to join a new ball of yarn – not a required skill, but helpful if you encounter a knot

Pattern Summary – with video links!

Getting Started

  1. Make a slip knot
    • Slip knot tutorial
  2. Chain (ch) 2
    • Chain stitch tutorial
  3. Single crochet (sc) into the first chain (2nd from the hook) [1 stitch]
    • Single crochet tutorial
  4. Ch 1, turn, and do a single crochet increase (2 sc in same stitch) [2 stitches]
    • How to crochet increases (Note: shown with a different stitch)

Increase to Center

  1. Ch 1, turn, sc across [2 stitches]
  2. Ch 1, turn, 2 sc in first stitch, sc across [3 stitches]
  3. Repeat steps 5 and 6 twenty-seven more times [30 stitches at widest point]

Decrease to End

  1. Ch 1, turn, sc until 2 stitches remain, sc2tog (single crochet two together) [29 stitches]
    • How to decrease in single crochet
  2. Ch 1, turn, sc across
  3. Repeat steps 8 and 9 twenty-seven more times [2 stitches remain]
  4. Ch 1, turn, sc2tog [1 stitch]
  5. Ch 1, turn, sc [1 stitch]

Border

  1. Ch 1, then sc around the edges of the bandana, placing 3 sc in the same st at the points of the border.
    • Note: If using a contrasting color, fasten off main color and attach new yarn.
    • How to add a single crochet border

Finishing

  1. Cut the yarn, leaving a 6” tail, and pull the tail through the final loop
  2. Weave in your ends
    • How to weave in ends


Back to Crochet Workshop Resources main page
